text:p.text:span: saying (the words) of Aradmu, your servant:
, captain of the royal armed forces, that my king sent me (a message) about;
my king must be informed about the matter.
When I focused my attention on
I was in the process of mustering troops for my king’s military service,
He had not gone to…, nor had he returned.
That crime was a very serious crime against my king!
Whatever you, my king order me to do, (I will do)!
Now my king is informed (about all of this)!
In some redactions of SEpM this composition takes the place of SEpM1. In all four extant manuscripts it is followed by SEpM2 or by its catch line (see p.
. I know of no other example of
; the translation is conjectural, based on
. I know of no other example of
in Sumerian. On the face of it, it should mean “royal expedition,” but even if it meant that earlier, OB times it was probably understood as a qalque from Akkadian
160/4 748, meant “royal military service.” See also J. G and A. Westenholz,

office:text.text:p: 11. The reading of the verb is conjectural; most probably this line is echoed—or echoes—ArŠ4 (8): 23.

text:p.text:span: No photograph is included here; the tablet will be published by

office:text.text:p: Tablet typology: Prism: N2, N5, Type III: N1, N3, N4.

text:p.text:span: a-ba-in-da-sá ugula éren zú-kešda lugal ní
N4 oo o . . . . àga-ús + . + o o . + . +
N5 .. + + + . o o o o o o o o o o o

office:text.text:p: N2 o o o o o o o o . . . . .

text:p.text:span: . . o o o o o o

office:text.text:p: N2 oo o o o o o o + + + - .
N3 ++ + + . . . o o o o o o
N4 o o o o o . . +
N4 o o o o o o o . +
N2 o o o o o o . + o . +

text:p.text:span: N3 + . + . . + o o o o o

office:text.text:p: N4 o o o o o o x x . . +

text:note-body.text:p: Only the very end of the final sign is preserved in both N1 and N2.

office:text.text:p: N1 o o . + . o o o x .
N2 o o o o o o o o o .
N3 . . . o o o . o o o
N2 o o o o o +

text:p.text:span: N1 o o . + o o o
N2 o o o o o . +

office:text.text:p: In N1 and N2 followed by SEpM2.
